title: Jessie Buckley Wins Best Actress Oscar for Hamnet
text: Jessie Buckley won the Best Actress Oscar at the 2026 Oscars for her role in Hamnet, completing a dominant awards run. Buckley earned frontrunner status from the moment Hamnet debuted at Telluride last fall, with her searing portrayal of wife and mother Agnes Shakespeare. Over the course of the last few months, Buckley picked up Best Actress honors at the Critics Choice Awards, the BAFTA Film Awards, and the Golden Globes. She had won a SAG, BAFTA and Golden Globe for the role in the Chloé Zhao movie about Agnes and William Shakespeare. Hamnet was nominated in eight total Academy Awards categories, including best picture, best director and best adapted screenplay. The film's biggest achievement came with Buckley's win. Buckley became the first Irish woman to win in the category. Reports had framed her as the outstanding favorite heading into Oscar night, with Hamnet also positioned as a major contender in several top categories, making the win feel less like a surprise and more like a coronation of an expected victor.
